ocr.text: The table presents findings on perceived access to care and barriers to care, categorized by the better condition with higher
scores and lower scores across two primary conditions: illicit drug use and hazardous drinking. For illicit drug use, ACME
shows a perceived access score of −0.17 (−0.35 to 0) and a barriers score of 0.06, with its proportion mediated at 0.11 (0 to

Hazardous drinking
ADE reports a perceived access score of −1.42 (−2.33 to −0.37) and a barriers score of < 0.01, with a proportion mediated value
of 0.49 (0.13 to 0.86) and a barrier score of 0.02. Total effects for illicit drug use yield a perceived access score of −1.59 (−2.58
to −0.53) with barriers at < 0.01 and a proportion mediated of 0.54 (0.18 to 0.92) with a barrier score of 0.01. For hazardous
drinking, ACME indicates a perceived access score of −0.24 (−0.35 to −0.15) with barriers at < 0.01 and a proportion mediated
of 0.06 (0.04 to 0.09) and < 0.01 barriers. ADE shows a perceived access score of −0.34 (−0.78 to 0.11) with barriers at 0.18
and a proportion mediated of 0.31 (0.17 to 0.45) with barriers at < 0.01. Total effects for hazardous drinking reflect a perceived
access score of −0.58 (−1.03 to −0.13) with barriers at 0.01 and a proportion mediated of 0.38 (0.23 to 0.51) with barriers at <
0.01. The overall proportions for hazardous drinking mediated are 0.42 (0.2 to 1.61) with barriers at 0.01 and a proportion
mediated of 0.16 (0.09 to 0.29) at < 0.01.
